data.title: Eagle Eye Networks Launches $1 Million 2026 Drako School Grant to Strengthen Security and Safety in Schools
data.description:
Eagle Eye Networks Launches $1 Million 2026 Drako School Grant to Strengthen Security and Safety in Schools

The 2026 Drako Smart Video Security Grant for Schools may be used for gun detection and camera sharing technology

Eagle Eye Networks, the global leader in cloud video surveillance, today launched the $1 million 2026 Drako Smart Video Security Grant for Schools, dedicated to strengthening safety and security for students, staff and community members in K-12 schools, colleges and universities.

Awardees may use the grant to implement Eagle Eye’s new AI Camera Gun Detection.

“We are on a mission at Eagle Eye to help schools change the way they use security cameras,” said Eagle Eye CEO Dean Drako. “Eagle Eye AI Gun Detection integrates with existing security cameras to detect brandished firearms in real-time, enabling proactive responses during an emergency.”

Grant recipients may receive up to one year of funding for the Eagle Eye Cloud VMS (video management system) and associated components. This smart video security solution includes security cameras, gun detection, 911 camera sharing, cloud storage, vape sensors, centralized management, and built-in analytics and AI features to help schools quickly address security issues and improve operations. Schools can use their existing security cameras, and have the option to integrate their on-site surveillance system into the Eagle Eye Cloud VMS.

Eagle Eye Networks has extensive experience protecting schools and universities around the world. Benefits for schools include:

  • Camera agnostic: Works with virtually any camera brand, including existing cameras. This means schools can easily upgrade their existing security system with advanced AI and cloud capabilities.
  • Eagle Eye Gun Detection: Transform existing security cameras from passive recording devices into a proactive gun detection system, a vital tool for prevention and protection. To deliver the highest possible accuracy, Eagle Eye engineered a Triple-layer Verification system that uses two levels of AI and one level of human verification.
  • Real-time insights for school administrators: Eagle Eye enhances your standard security cameras with AI, which gives those cameras the ability to detect threats, improve situational awareness, and provide timely information.
  • Eagle Eye 911 Camera Sharing: Designate cameras to be securely, automatically shared with emergency response centers to give first responders vital information during a school emergency.
  • Cybersecurity and privacy: Bank-level cybersecurity, triple redundancy, camera lockdown and privacy protections are built into Eagle Eye products.
  • Comprehensive security solution: Eagle Eye capabilities include vape detection and other sensors, license plate recognition, and cabinet systems for remote or hard to wire locations. Eagle Eye also integrates with door access control, and an unlimited number of third-party technologies delivering a holistic solution for schools and campuses of any size.

This is the third time that Eagle Eye Networks has funded a Drako Smart Video Security Grants for Schools. More than $2 million has been awarded to U.S. schools through this grant.

Applications are being accepted starting today. Grants will be awarded on an ongoing basis. To learn more and apply for the grant, please visit Eagle Eye Networks Drako Smart Video Security Grant for Schools.

ABOUT EAGLE EYE NETWORKS

Eagle Eye Networks is the global leader in cloud video surveillance, delivering cyber-secure, cloud-based video with artificial intelligence (AI) and analytics to make businesses more efficient and the world a safer place. Businesses of all sizes utilize the Eagle Eye Cloud VMS (video management system) to centralize their video surveillance and obtain better security and operations. Purpose-built for the cloud and AI, the Eagle Eye Cloud VMS addresses customers’ security and operational needs with unlimited scalability, simple usage-based subscription pricing, advanced analytics, integrated AI, and an open RESTful API platform delivering flexibility. Eagle Eye sells through a global network of resellers and integrators. Founded in 2012, Eagle Eye is headquartered in Austin, Texas, with offices in Amsterdam, Bangalore, and Tokyo. Learn more at www.een.com.

EAGLE EYE PRESS CONTACT
GLOBAL HQ
Martha Entwistle
mentwistle@een.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251113729950/en/

data.title: CSG Launches ChatCSG with New Federal Contract Giving America’s Leaders Powerful New Insights on National Security
data.description:
CSG Launches ChatCSG with New Federal Contract Giving America’s Leaders Powerful New Insights on National Security

Capital Solutions Group, LLC, a leading provider of artificial intelligence, cloud, and cybersecurity solutions for defense and intelligence missions, today announced it has been awarded a sole-source contract to deliver a highly customized generative AI solution to a federal customer. The contract award also marks the official launch of the company’s proprietary large-language-model platform, ChatCSG, a secure, mission-ready AI environment engineered for national security and defense applications.

Under the new agreement, CSG will deploy an innovative AI solution that transforms how teams access and interact with critical information by enabling secure, natural-language questions and contextually accurate responses drawn from trusted organizational knowledge. The platform enhances collaboration, streamlines knowledge sharing, and delivers actionable intelligence to government leaders - empowering faster, more informed decision-making on vital matters.

“This project is a milestone moment for CSG,” said Jeff May, CEO of CSG. “ChatCSG represents exactly what differentiates us in the highly competitive national security market. We’re not simply integrating existing AI tools — we’re designing proprietary, mission-specific capabilities that address the federal government’s toughest information challenges. Our team’s depth in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and cloud engineering allows us to deliver AI that’s explainable, traceable, and responsibly built for classified environments.”

Introducing ChatCSG™

ChatCSG is a proprietary generative AI platform designed and developed by CSG to meet the rigorous demands of national security and defense environments. Built on a retrieval-augmented LLM architecture, the product enables users to pose secure, natural-language queries and receive precise, data-driven answers drawn directly from verified internal knowledge sources.

Key differentiators include:

  • Model Context Protocol Integration: Implements the emerging Model Context Protocol (MCP) standard for machine-native APIs, enabling secure, structured interaction across tools, datasets, and systems.
  • Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): References authoritative datasets before generating responses to minimize “hallucinations.”
  • Real-Time Integration: Leverages internal/external APIs and tooling in a manner consistent with today's standards.
  • Responsible AI Compliance: Incorporates DoD-mandated Responsible AI (RAI) frameworks, Chief Digital & Artificial Intelligence Office (CDAO) guardrails, and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
  • Tailored Security and Auditability: Traceable, explainable AI outputs designed for sensitive operations.

CSG’s proprietary AI platform is already being customized to accelerate cybersecurity mission operations, threat detection, and knowledge management for defense and intelligence teams.

This award is the third major milestone in recent months for CSG, following its joint-venture Patrius LLC’s intelligence-community contract win and the appointment of national security technology leader Larry Denton as chief operating and growth officer. The company plans to expand its footprint across the defense and intelligence sectors, investing in proprietary IP and emerging AI technologies to strengthen U.S. mission readiness.

About Capital Solutions Group, LLC

Capital Solutions Group delivers mission-first advanced technology solutions for defense and intelligence agencies. With deep roots in the national security technology community, Capital Solutions Group understands the mission protocols, challenges and security demands that govern government systems. The company develops proprietary products and integrates advanced commercial technologies—specifically cloud, AI/ML and cybersecurity—into secure, mission-ready solutions from the edge to enterprise scale. Founded in 2007, Capital Solutions Group is a rapidly growing small business with offices in Annapolis and Columbia, Maryland. Learn more at www.capitalsolutionsgroup.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

Media Contact:
Kristina Messner (for CSG)
kristina@messnermediagroup.com
+1-703-716-3181

data.title: The U.S. official who is most serious about -2-
data.description:
Markets don't believe the Fed is serious about inflation

In her interview with MarketWatch, Hammack said she is keeping an eye on the labor market, alongside her focus on inflation, and is not trying to push the economy into a "negative situation." She added that she wouldn't be as concerned about high prices if inflation were just one-tenth or two-tenths away from the 2% target. "But right now we're at 3%," she said.

Hammack said she's worried that in the financial market, traders and investors are starting to believe the Fed isn't serious about its 2% inflation target.

"If you read some of the market participant chatter, some of them are talking about, well, maybe what the Fed means by its target is just below 3%, and that getting it all the way to 2% is not a priority," she said. "To me, getting it back to 2% is critical for our credibility, and that's our objective."

The difference between 2% and 3% is not trivial: It means inflation is 50% higher than monetary policy makers want it to be.

In a speech to the Economic Club of New York last week, Hammack said she thinks the economy will pick up next year. She pointed to the booming stock market and easing credit conditions in the lending market. With their ability to tap both equity and debt markets, she expects businesses to invest and economic growth to pick up in 2026 and eventually push down the unemployment rate.

"I do not currently put high odds of a labor-market downturn," Hammack said, adding that lower rates would likely just fuel higher prices. "At this point, I don't think there is more that monetary policy can do."

Steven Stanley, chief economist at banking giant Santander Group, said that Fed officials like Hammack who are worried about higher inflation won't want to cut rates until they see inflation trending down.

But Rosengren said that with inflation higher than the Fed wants and the labor market weaker, "the possibility that the Fed makes a mistake at this juncture is probably uncomfortably high."

Officials are divided. Fed governor Stephen Miran, who is in his current position while on leave from his post as Trump's top economist, has said that concerns about inflation are overblown and that the economy could slip into a recession without more rate cuts.

For her part, Hammack said she will walk into every Fed interest-rate committee meeting with an open mind.

"I want to hear from people who think differently from me. I want to understand their perspective. I want to know what I'm missing," she said. "It's a very complicated $30 trillion economy. We can all disagree on exactly where we are."

-Greg Robb

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-13-25 0900ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: The U.S. official who is most serious about fighting inflation is in Cleveland
data.description:
By Greg Robb

The rise in consumer prices is on track to exceed the central bank's target until 2028. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack is trying to get that inflation rate down.

"At some point that has to stop": Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack is laser-focused on inflation.

Part of what drives Beth Hammack's relentless focus on inflation are the conversations she's had with people who live near her office. As president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland, Hammack is part of the U.S. central-banking system, which has 12 regional banks across the country that help it gather the economic data upon which monetary-policy decisions are based.

Some of the data Hammack collects are what's known in the economics trade as soft data - anecdotes, stories, opinions and sentiment. Hammack's region is made up of Ohio, western Pennsylvania, eastern Kentucky and parts of West Virginia, and when she goes to these areas, she said she hears about one thing more than any other - the impact of high prices.

"When you talk with these low- and moderate-income families, people who are [paid] hourly, they are really struggling," Hammack said in an interview with MarketWatch. "They go from steak to ground beef, ground beef to hot dogs to beans, and at some point you're just having pasta or visiting the food bank."

Hammack has been hearing these stories ever since she left Wall Street and her job as co-head of the global financing group at Goldman Sachs and was appointed in 2024 to run the Cleveland Fed.

"Most of their paycheck is going to housing, food and transportation," said Hammack. "At some point that has to stop."

By its own measure, the Federal Reserve is failing at its stated goal of keeping prices from rising by more than 2% annually. In the 12 months ending in September, consumer prices rose by 3%. Inflation has been higher than the Fed's target since March 2021, and the central bank now expects the rise in prices to continue to exceed its 2% target until 2028. That would represent almost a decade of failure.

The Fed's key tool for fighting inflation has traditionally been to increase benchmark interest rates in order to slow the economy down. But this fall it has done the opposite, cutting rates by 50 basis points. Several Fed officials and President Donald Trump have been pushing for more cuts, and Trump is widely expected to appoint a new Fed chair in May 2026 who will lead the charge to lower rates even more.

Still, given the way the Federal Reserve votes on whether to cut rates, several Fed officials could ultimately make their own call. Hammack has emerged as the central bank's strongest voice trying to protect Americans from higher prices. She lands on the MarketWatch 25 list of the people reshaping the way we live, work, save, spend and invest.

In their decisions, Fed policy makers are guided by the central bank's dual mandate to achieve maximum employment while also keeping prices down. In an interview with MarketWatch, Hammack said she understood why some of her colleagues are concerned about the employment piece of the Fed's mission, given recent sluggishness in the labor market. However, she's not taking her focus away from prices.

"I am worried about the labor market," Hammack said. But looking at the balance between the labor market and inflation, "we've got this persistent high inflation that is sticking around, when all is said and done it will be the better part of a decade."

Emerging as a strong voice

Beth Hammack spent three decades at Goldman Sachs, the nation's pre-eminent investment bank. On Wall Street, if you are wrong for the better part of a decade, it doesn't matter if time ultimately proves you right. Nobody can afford to wait eight years to get something right. That's the mentality Hammack brings as she becomes a voting member next year on the Federal Open Market Committee, which sets benchmark interest rates.

Hammack said she doesn't support any more interest-rate cuts unless circumstances change. Trump has repeatedly said he'd like to see rates cut, and whoever he picks to take over as Fed chair next year will likely share that view. That means Hammack will be facing an uphill battle in her effort to keep prices down.

'They go from steak to ground beef, ground beef to hot dogs to beans, and at some point you're just having pasta or visiting the food bank.'Beth Hammack, president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland

One reason Trump has said he wants to lower interest rates is to help fund the federal debt. Lowering the benchmark rate would likely lower the interest rates on the notes and bonds the U.S. government sells to finance its budget deficit - at the very least those with shorter maturities. The government must keep borrowing, because it spends more than it collects in revenue.

Hammack is an expert in debt management, having served for five years, while at Goldman, as chair of the committee of Wall Street traders who advise the Treasury secretary on the government's debt-management strategy.

Fed officials want to stick to their mandate of keeping the labor market strong and inflation down, and they will likely rely on Hammack's expertise to counter arguments from the administration that the central bank should play a role in curbing the government deficit.

"If you're worried about the costs of deficits, then you should bring the deficit down, which means either cut government spending or increase taxes. That's in the purview of the administration and Congress to do. They don't need the Fed to do it," said former Boston Fed President Eric Rosengren. Trying to minimize the cost of deficits through interest rates is something Third World countries do, he added.

The pressure to cut isn't only coming from Trump, however. On Wall Street, investors are now expecting that the new Fed chair will be forceful in bringing rates down. That so-called market sentiment has helped push up the prices of just about every asset in 2025 - U.S. stocks, foreign stocks, bonds and gold.

But reality is different from market sentiment. Economists and former Fed officials say the next leader of the central bank likely won't have the votes on the 12-member interest-rate committee to push a dramatic rate-cutting agenda. It takes seven members to make a policy decision. Last summer, there was concern that Trump-appointed members of the Fed board would try to replace Fed district presidents on the committee who didn't agree with a lower-rate agenda, but that concern has eased.

The new chair will have to craft policy that incorporates the thinking of a number of regional Fed presidents, including Hammack, who have inflation concerns that are unlikely to be easily allayed.

"It's going to be an interesting period to watch the dynamics of the committee," Rosengren said. "Inflation is going to be fairly sticky, and someone like Hammack who is concerned about getting inflation back to 2% over a reasonable period of time is going to be concerned, and presumably vocal, as a voting member."

Right now, Hammack is part of a bloc of roughly nine officials who appear to be more focused on tackling the inflation problem.

"At the moment, Hammack is one voice of a pretty large bloc of hawkish officials. She's representing that bloc pretty well," said Matthew Luzzetti, chief U.S. economist at Deutsche Bank. "If the labor market stabilizes or even rebounds, then I think you will see other officials move more in line with Hammack's view that there could be limited scope to cut rates next year."

The relentless rise in prices

Americans are paying higher prices for services - everyday things like electric bills and insurance - with increases running above 3%. Most Fed officials think that some of the higher inflation currently affecting consumers and businesses is coming from Trump's tariff policies, but for the most part, they see it as a one-time boost in prices that will soon fade.

Hammack disagrees. She notes that a lot of inflation pressure is coming from services and not from imported goods. "It's hard for me to see that core services [aside from] housing is being driven by tariffs," she said.

However, she thinks that inflation from tariffs, which has so far been more muted than many economists anticipated, could also spike early in 2026. "There are a lot of contracts to get renegotiated when you get to the beginning of the year. And so it could be that we see pricing come through," she said.

'If you're worried about the costs of deficits, then you should bring the deficit down, which means either cut government spending or increase taxes. That's in the purview of the administration and Congress to do. They don't need the Fed to do it.'Eric Rosengren, former president, Federal Reserve Bank of Boston

When consumer inflation spiked to 7% in the summer of 2022, the Fed raised interest rates above 5%. The aggressive move from the U.S. central bank helped bring inflation down from 7% to 3% without causing an economic recession. But that did not bring the absolute price of goods and services down - it just slowed the rise in prices from extremely high to merely high levels.

In the fall of 2024, shortly after Hammack took up her new post, the Fed cut rates by 1 percentage point over three meetings. Hammack was a voting member on the Fed's interest-rate setting committee at the time. She dissented from the last cut, of a quarter-point in December 2024, after supporting the first two, which were a half-point and a quarter-point, respectively.

"We had a line of sight to inflation coming down toward our target. We'd gone from above 7 to below 3%. We were headed in the right direction," Hammack said.

Looking back, Rosengren said the Fed initially appeared on track to effectively combat inflation in 2025 as it slowly started moving in the direction of the Fed's 2% inflation target. But the tariffs announced by the Trump administration in April of this year derailed those efforts, he said.

(MORE TO FOLLOW) Dow Jones Newswires

11-13-25 0900ET

Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: ImageKit launches its MCP server, enabling secure AI assistant integration to accelerate bulk media operations and automate workflows
data.description:
ImageKit launches its MCP server, enabling secure AI assistant integration to accelerate bulk media operations and automate workflows

Teams can input natural language into their AI assistants to access ImageKit API functionality for faster and automated media workflows.

ImageKit, a unified image and video API platform with integrated Digital Asset Management (DAM), today announced the launch of its Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server, enabling developers and creative teams to interact with ImageKit APIs through their favorite AI assistants or other MCP-compatible clients. By providing a standardized and secure way to access ImageKit APIs, teams can now automate media processing and management, creating intelligent workflows without manually using the ImageKit dashboard or writing code.

Users can choose to connect through an OAuth-hosted remote MCP server, managed and maintained by ImageKit, to reduce operational overhead, or deploy the MCP server locally for enhanced security and greater configuration control.

“From development and design to marketing and sales, AI assistants are rapidly becoming integral to how teams work today,” said Manu Chaudhary, co-founder & CTO, ImageKit. “Our MCP server extends ImageKit’s core capabilities into this new AI ecosystem, enabling secure, intelligent, and context-aware access to media operations and transformations from within popular tools like Claude or Cursor. It’s about giving end users the power of ImageKit through the same interfaces where they already think, code, or create.”

Unlock smarter, faster media workflows with ImageKit’s MCP Server

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that enables AI applications to interact with APIs in a secure and standardized way through natural-language prompts. With ImageKit’s MCP Server, teams can now use assistants like Claude, Cursor, or Windsurf to complete a wide range of media tasks, from updating tags to transforming assets, simply by prompting them. Every operation happens securely through ImageKit’s APIs, turning manual, time-consuming work into intelligent, automated workflows.

Here’s how teams benefit:

  • Automate repetitive media tasks: Instantly create folders, search files, and update tags, add metadata, and add or remove tags across files without manually using the dashboard.
  • Streamline creative production: Resize, crop, remove backgrounds, or apply transformations for media assets directly through AI assistants.
  • Account insights and reporting: Retrieve media library storage details, number of extensions used, video processing units consumed, and bandwidth usage, all with a single prompt.

ImageKit has been steadily integrating AI-powered capabilities into its image and video API and Digital Asset Management (DAM) platform, enabling users to perform advanced media transformations such as generative fill, smart cropping for images and videos, background replacement, and AI-based image generation. Also, ImageKit’s new image editor enables users to bulk edit images effortlessly, combining multiple transformations, previewing changes in real time, and instantly generating ready-to-use image URLs or downloadable final versions.

With ImageKit’s new MCP Server that connects ImageKit APIs to the AI ecosystem, your AI assistant can now become a powerful extension of your media team, automating workflows, boosting productivity, and helping you deliver flawless visuals faster than ever.

About ImageKit

ImageKit.io is a unified Image and Video API combined with an AI-powered Digital Asset Management (DAM) platform. It empowers developers and marketers to deliver flawless visual experiences across the web. Offering automatic media optimization, over 50 real-time transformations, and superfast content delivery via a global CDN, ImageKit simplifies how you handle and deliver images and videos. Its integrated DAM further enhances asset management, visual search, and team collaboration. Trusted by over 250,000 developers and 2,000+ businesses globally, ImageKit enables developers to ship faster, marketers to iterate freely, and your users to enjoy flawless visuals everywhere.

Serena Job
serena@imagekit.io

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251111080429/en/

data.title: Circle's stock closed at worst level since its first day of trade. Now the stablecoin issuer gets a double upgrade from JPMorgan
data.description:
By Steve Goldstein

Most other analysts will be moving their price targets in the opposite direction

(L-R) Jeremy Allaire, chief executive of Circle Internet Financial Ltd., and World Bank President Ajay Banga speak during "The Future of Finance" seminar in Washington, D.C., on Oct. 14, 2025.

After closing at its lowest level since its first day of trade, Circle Internet Group Inc. saw a double upgrade from JPMorgan.

Despite the 12% share-price slide after third-quarter results, analysts led by Kenneth Worthington called the results "solid" with key metrics exceeding expectations.

The analysts nudged their Circle (CRCL) price target to $100 from $94, above Tuesday's close of $86.30.

Most other analysts will be moving their price targets in the opposite direction. According to FactSet, the average target is $158.24.

"The quarterly results indicate to us that stablecoins are continuing to make their way into mainstream financial services, with USDC a leading stablecoin and Circle a leading partner," the JPMorgan analysts said while upgrading the stock to overweight from underweight.

The analysts did concede that USDC's (USDCUSD) market share is flattening out as Tether (USDTUSD) is growing and Circle is investing heavily in its network.

"A key unknown for us remains whether stablecoins will be a winner-take-most-market - an answer here will be important in gauging future gross margins for Circle," they said.

-Steve Goldstein

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  11-13-25 0849ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Here's an unexpected twist that could throw the stock-market rally off course next year
data.description:
By Barbara Kollmeyer

Investors may be a little too confident over a Goldilocks scenario

The stock market could veer off course, warns a strategist.

Now that the government shutdown has officially ended, the next order of business will be a backlog of economic data. Some wariness seems to be setting in as investors ponder that.

The year has been marked by one lingering worry - that the stock market has run up too far too fast, largely due to a handful of stocks exposed to the hot AI trade. While Wednesday showed a burst of strength for out-of-favor value stocks, fear of missing out on a stock market rally has kept investors in the market despite their valuation concerns.

Our call of the day, from Nomura cross-asset strategist Charlie McElligott, flags one risk he sees as overlooked that could throw a monkey wrench in the stock rally - economic strength that backs the Fed into an unexpected corner.

"It still seems to me that the scenario which would catch the max # of people 'flat-footed' is a situation where most continue to anticipate 'modest cooling' that allows for 'smooth Fed easing glidepath' to buffer the economy," he writes in a note to clients.

McElligott notes there's plenty of belief out there in a so-called Goldilocks-type scenario - investors remain confident that the Fed can ease, while consumers keep spending and wages stay strong enough.

But he raises the idea of a twist where the economy takes a surprising turn - higher growth, rising inflation - which "forces the market to price in a meaningfully 'less dovish' Fed path, i.e. de facto TIGHTENING."

So few investors right now might be prepared for a Fed that wants to keep interest rates higher or even increase them, he said. Granted, the state of the economy remains in question, which has made December's Fed meeting something of a toss-up - the CME's FedWatch Tool showed the odds of a December rate cut are now around 55%.

By the end of 2026, however, traders expect the fed funds rate to range between 3% and 3.25%, from a range of 3.75% to 4% currently.

But not only are there loose financial conditions to the support the economy, there's the positive wealth effect that comes from the rising stock market, he says.

Another issue he flags is that as companies spend on AI, they'll have left cash to fund stock buybacks.

Goldman Sachs strategists already have noted that AI hyperscalers use more of their cash on capital expenditure than stock buybacks.

The markets

U.S. stock futures (ES00) (YM00) (NQ00) are pushing lower, as the yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y is rising. Silver (SI00) is set for its fifth-straight winning session after another record on Wednesday. 

   Key asset performance                                                Last       5d      1m      YTD      1y 
   S&P 500                                                              6850.92    0.80%   2.70%   16.48%   14.46% 
   Nasdaq Composite                                                     23,406.46  -0.40%  3.25%   21.21%   21.71% 
   10-year Treasury                                                     4.096      0.80    12.30   -48.00   -34.70 
   Gold                                                                 4219.7     5.89%   -2.87%  59.88%   64.16% 
   Oil                                                                  58.43      -1.85%  2.60%   -18.70%  -14.85% 
   Data: MarketWatch. Treasury yields change expressed in basis points

Need to Know starts early and is updated until the opening bell, but sign up here to get it delivered once to your email box. The emailed version will be sent out at about 7:30 a.m. Eastern.

Take control of your news. Make MarketWatch your preferred source on Google.

The buzz

The government shutdown is over after President Donald Trump signed a temporary funding bill into law that was passed earlier Wednesday by the House. Next up is the data backup that should restart soon.

Cisco shares (CSCO) are jumping on a strong earnings beat thanks to AI demand for the networking equipment and services group products.

Disney (DIS) shares are slipping after the entertainment giant's revenue slightly disappointed and operating income fell.

Nike stock (NKE) is up after a reported upgrade to overweight from equal weight at Wells Fargo.

Applied Materials (AMAT) earnings due after the close.

A speech from St. Louis Fed Pres. Alberto Musalem is expected around 12:15 p.m.

Best of the web

Michael Burry de-registers his hedge fund. 'Onto much better things' he says.

China authorities are hunting tax-dodging sellers on Amazon and e-commerce sites.

The secret meeting that ended the shutdown.

The chart

Citadel Securities offers this chart showing retail investors buying Magnificent 7 stocks in November, notably as the tech sector took a hit as the month kicked off. The chart is part of a new Citadel tool used to track whether investors are increasing or decreasing exposure to varied forms of market risk - what it calls the Retail Risk On/Risk Off (Retail RORO) measure. Citadel's head of equity and equity derivatives strategy Scott Rubner says the week ending Nov. 10 saw the fifth strongest retail dip-buying overall in the past two years. "Despite pervasive macro anxiety, positioning and flows tell a different story. Retail and corporate demand continue to underpin markets, suggesting the year-end rally is delayed-not derailed," says Rubner.

Top tickers

These were the top-searched tickers on MarketWatch as of 6 a.m.: 

   Ticker  Security name 
   NVDA    Nvidia 
   TSLA    Tesla 
   AMD     Advanced Micro Devices 
   PLTR    Palantir Technologies 
   GME     GameStop 
   META    Meta 
   TSM     Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing 
   AMZN    Amazon 
   AAPL    Apple 
   BYND    Beyond Meat

Random reads

A pricey "cut up sock." Apple's new $230 iPhone Pocket gets roasted.

Bagpipers clinch world record jamming to AC/DC hit.

Boom time for U.K. treasure seekers

-Barbara Kollmeyer

data.title: Global Medical REIT Inc. Announces Proposed Public Offering of Series B Preferred Stock
data.description:
Global Medical REIT Inc. Announces Proposed Public Offering of Series B Preferred Stock

Global Medical REIT Inc. (NYSE:GMRE) (the “Company”) today announced the commencement of an underwritten public offering of shares of its Series B Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock, $0.001 par value per share (“Series B Preferred Stock”), subject to market and other conditions. The Company expects to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase additional shares of Series B Preferred Stock to cover over-allotments, if any. The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, which may include funding new acquisitions and repaying indebtedness, including amounts outstanding under the Company’s credit facility.

The Company intends to apply to list the Series B Preferred Stock on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol “GMRE-PrB.”

Raymond James, BMO Capital Markets, Stifel and Baird are serving as the book-running managers for the offering.

The offering is being made pursuant to the Company’s shelf registration statement, as amended, which was declared effective by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (“SEC”) on April 4, 2024. This press release shall not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy nor shall there be any sale of these shares or any other securities in any state in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful, prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any state.

About Global Medical REIT Inc.

Global Medical REIT Inc. is a net-lease medical REIT that acquires healthcare facilities and leases those facilities to physician groups and regional and national healthcare systems.

data.title: Major international logistics firm signs for 80,000 sqm of space at new CTPark Budapest Érd in Hungary
data.description:
Major international logistics firm signs for 80,000 sqm of space at new CTPark Budapest Érd in Hungary

Regulatory News:

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251113583809/en/

CTP, Europe’s largest listed owner, developer and manager of logistics and industrial real estate by gross lettable area, announces a major international logistics business has leased 80,000 sqm of BTS space for a major distribution hub at the new CTPark Budapest–Érd in Hungary.

The new 80,000 sqm BTS logistics building will be delivered to the tenant during Q1 2027. The logistics operator has signed a long-term lease for an 80,000 sqm hub that will enable it to consolidate its operations into a single, large-scale, modern facility designed to meet its specific requirements. The business chose CTPark Budapest–Érd for its prime location, direct access to M6 highway, excellent accessibility, and local workforce. The Érd and Budafok-Tétény regions are close to the Danube River and Hungary’s M0, M1, M7 and M6 motorways. CTPark Budapest–Érd represents CTP’s sixteenth industrial and logistics park in Hungary.

CTP started to develop the new park in January 2025, on 70 hectares of land on the border of the Érd and Budafok-Tétény regions near Budapest. CTPark Budapest–Érd will eventually provide overall 250,000 sqm of modern, sustainable Grade-A space. The recently signed landmark deal of 80,000 sqm and the first phase’s 75,000 sqm GLA is a clear indicator of strong pre-let interest in the submarket from occupiers, seeing opportunity in the high quality I&L space they know CTP will deliver, and because of the park’s location with access to a large skilled local labour pool and supported with direct highway access and excellent transport links to the rest of Hungary and other European markets.

The park will include additional service facilities, all within attractively landscaped green spaces and amenity. Pedestrian and bicycle routes have been prioritised in the park’s design, including a new connection to the local Tétényliget railway station and direct access to the EuroVelo 6 Danube-side cycling route.

All buildings at CTPark Budapest–Érd will incorporate the latest sustainable technology. Energy efficient features will include thermal insulation, LED lighting, and PV panels supporting long-term sustainable performance. Green façades will enhance biodiversity and elevate the park’s design. CTPark Budapest–Érd has also been designed to fit seamlessly into the surrounding riverside landscape. CTP has planted 2.5 hectares of new forest along the Danube, while a strategic partnership with the Hungarian Garden Heritage Foundation will help ensure the Park incorporates a wider array of sustainable features.

“Sustainability, innovation, and social responsibility are at the heart of CTP’s philosophy. With every project we aim to create long-term environmental and economic value, in cooperation with local municipalities, with focus on innovative construction and long-term quality asset management. Our approach to developing CTPark Budapest–Érd has been the very same. The Park has sustainable design at its heart, and we’ve worked closely with local municipalities and other stakeholder as partners to support community initiatives as we look to strengthen the local economy and contribute to the region’s wider economic growth,” said Dr. Ferenc Gondi, Managing Director of CTP Hungary.

CTP operates Central and Eastern Europe’s most extensive business park network. The company is active in ten markets with many of its clients occupying space at multiple CTParks in different countries. Over two thirds of new leases CTP signs every year are with existing clients that choose to expend across its network of business parks.

About CTP

CTP is Europe’s largest listed owner, developer, and operator of logistics and industrial real estate by gross leasable area, with 13.5 million sqm of GLA across 10 countries as of 30 June 2025. CTP certifies all new buildings to BREEAM Very good or better and earned a negligible-risk ESG rating by Sustainalytics, underlining its commitment to being a sustainable business. For more information, visit CTP’s corporate website: www.ctp.eu

data.title: Protagenic Therapeutics Announces Completion of Enrollment and Dosing in Phase 1 MAD Study
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / November 13, 2025 / Protagenic Therapeutics Inc. (NASDAQ:PTIX), a biopharmaceutical company developing novel therapeutics for stress-related neuropsychiatric and neurodegenerative disorders, today announced the successful completion of enrollment and dosing in its Multiple Ascending Dose (MAD) Phase 1 study evaluating its lead compound, known as PT00114. The study, conducted in healthy volunteers, was designed to assess the safety and tolerability profile of PT00114 following multiple doses over time. With all healthy volunteers now dosed, the company has achieved a milestone in the clinical development of its proprietary peptide-based therapeutic.

"We've completed all dosing for all healthy volunteers in the Phase 1 Study," said Dr. Garo Armen, Executive Chairman of Protagenic Therapeutics. "We plan to deliver a full summation of our analysis of the safety results within by the end of the month."

The company expects to finalize and release top-line results from the MAD study by November 30th.

"This milestone underscores our commitment to advancing PT00114 as a novel, first-in-class therapeutic aimed at restoring resilience and balance in individuals suffering from chronic stress and its neurological consequences," added Dr. Armen. "The company had previously completed a single ascending dose study in early 2025. We are encouraged by the progress to date and are excited to move toward the next phase of development."

About Protagenic Therapeutics, Inc.

Protagenic Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:PTIX) is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative peptide-based therapeutics targeting the biological pathways underlying stress-related neurological and mood disorders. The company's lead compound, PT00114, is a synthetic analog of a naturally occurring brain peptide that helps regulate the body's stress response and emotional equilibrium. For more information, visit www.protagenic.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

Statements in this press release contain "forward-looking statements," within the meaning of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, that are subject to substantial risks and uncertainties. All statements, other than statements of historical fact, contained in this press release are forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements contained in this press release may be identified by the use of words such as "anticipate," "believe," "contemplate," "could," "estimate," "expect," "intend," "seek," "may," "might," "plan," "potential," "predict," "project," "suggest," "target," "aim," "should," "will," "would," or the negative of these words or other similar expressions, although not all forward-looking statements contain these words. Forward-looking statements are based on the Company's current expectations and are subject to inherent uncertainties, risks and assumptions that are difficult to predict, including risks related to the completion, timing and results of current and future clinical studies relating to the Company's product candidates. Further, certain forward-looking statements are based on assumptions as to future events that may not prove to be accurate. These and other risks and uncertainties are described more fully in the section titled "Risk Factors" in the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Forward-looking statements contained in this announcement are made as of this date, and the Company undertakes no duty to update such information except as required under applicable law.
